description_long: This is a robust, classical-style stone vase with a wide, rounded opening and a thick, decorative rim featuring a series of bulbous, bead-like protrusions. The body of the vase is adorned with an ornate garland motif, likely representing laurel or oak leaves, which wraps around the upper section and is anchored by a central rosette. The surface shows signs of weathering, with subtle discoloration and pitting, suggesting it has been exposed to the elements. The vase sits atop a solid, circular pedestal base that provides stability and elevates the vessel. Its overall form is reminiscent of traditional garden urns often found in European parks or courtyards. The interior appears hollow, suitable for holding soil, plants, or decorative arrangements.
description: A weathered, classical stone vase with a beaded rim, ornate garland relief, and a sturdy pedestal base, ideal for outdoor display.
description_view_0: Front view showing the garland motif and central rosette detail prominently, with the beaded rim and pedestal base clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the continuous garland relief and the curvature of the vase, with less emphasis on the central rosette.
description_view_2: Another frontal angle with the garland and rosette visible, showing more surface texture and weathering on the body.
description_view_3: Angled view focusing on the garland wrapping around the vase, with the rim and base in clear profile.
